content_markdown:
P and Q are two points on a standing wave. R and S are two points on a progressive (travelling) wave.

Which of the following gives the relationship between the amplitudes of each pair of points?

diagram_metadata.description: Two wave diagrams. The first shows a standing wave with two loops (nodes and antinodes) and points P and Q located at different positions within the same loop. The second shows a progressive wave with points R and S located at different horizontal positions along the wave profile.

options.label: A
options.text: Points P and Q: same amplitude; Points R and S: same amplitude

options.label: B
options.text: Points P and Q: different amplitude; Points R and S: same amplitude

options.label: C
options.text: Points P and Q: same amplitude; Points R and S: different amplitude

options.label: D
options.text: Points P and Q: different amplitude; Points R and S: different amplitude

vibe_explanation: In a standing wave, the amplitude varies depending on the position (distance from a node), so points P and Q at different positions have different amplitudes. In a progressive wave, every point on the wave eventually reaches the same maximum displacement (amplitude) as the wave travels through them, so R and S have the same amplitude.
